ontology.foodex2_description: Bread and similar products — leavened flat bread (focaccia); mapped under FoodEx2 bread category as closest defensible anchor
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a dedicated focaccia code; A000E (bread and similar products) used as the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for focaccia bread; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving of focaccia bread is approximately one slice (60 g), which provides around 184 kcal, 5.8 g protein, 4.8 g fat, and 28.9 g carbohydrate.

nutrient_source.title: Focaccia Bread — Singapore Health Promotion Board (HPB) Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Focaccia bread (cr_id F05030006), confidence 0.95. All core macros and secondary nutrients are taken from this canonical HPB record.

gi_source.title: International table of glycemic index and glycemic load values — white wheat bread and leavened flatbreads
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. Diabetes Care 2008
gi_source.source_match_notes: Focaccia bread GI of 75 is sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for white wheat focaccia / leavened Italian flatbread. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (75 × 28.9) / 100 = 21.7 for a 60 g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 642 mg per 100 g — exceeds the 600 mg/100 g upper threshold for hypertension-sensitive foods
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit focaccia bread to a small slice (30–40 g) per occasion and seek reduced-salt versions prepared with no more than 1 g salt per 100 g dough, as the characteristic high-salt preparation contributes substantially to daily sodium load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High GI of 75 and 48.2 g carbohydrate per 100 g with only 2.7 g dietary fibre — refined white wheat flour base drives rapid postprandial glucose rise
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should restrict focaccia to a small portion (one thin slice, ~40 g), pair it with protein and non-starchy vegetables to blunt glycaemic response, and prefer wholegrain or high-fibre versions where available to reduce the glycaemic impact.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated sodium (642 mg/100 g) raises cardiovascular risk; saturated fat is low (1.2 g/100 g) and olive oil base provides monounsaturated fatty acids
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For cardiovascular disease management, the primary concern with focaccia is its high sodium content; limit portion size to 30–40 g per serving, choose olive-oil-based preparations over butter-enriched variants, and account for focaccia's sodium contribution within the daily 1500–2000 mg cardiovascular sodium budget.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Olive oil and rosemary provide anti-inflammatory polyphenols (oleocanthal); however, high sodium and high glycaemic load may promote systemic inflammation
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Focaccia prepared with extra-virgin olive oil and rosemary offers anti-inflammatory polyphenol benefits for rheumatoid arthritis, but the high sodium (642 mg/100 g) and high glycaemic load can worsen tissue swelling and systemic inflammation — limit to a small slice and prefer wholegrain versions.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Focaccia bread is a traditional Italian leavened flatbread originating from the Liguria region of Italy. It is made from a simple dough of wheat flour, water, yeast, salt, and a generous quantity of olive oil, which is both incorporated into the dough and brushed liberally over the surface before baking. The bread is characteristically dimpled by pressing fingers into the dough prior to baking, allowing olive oil to pool in the indentations. It is typically topped with coarse sea salt, rosemary, and other dried or fresh herbs. The texture is soft and chewy on the inside with a lightly crisp, golden crust on the outside, and the flavour is rich, savoury, and faintly herbal with a pronounced olive oil character. Per 100 g, focaccia provides 307 kcal, 10.6 g protein, 8.0 g fat (predominantly monounsaturated from olive oil), 48.2 g carbohydrate, 2.7 g dietary fibre, and 642 mg sodium. The high sodium content (642 mg/100 g) makes it unsuitable for individuals with hypertension or cardiovascular disease unless portion-controlled. Its high glycaemic index (GI 75) and moderate-to-low fibre content make it a high-glycaemic food that requires careful portion management for people with Type 2 Diabetes. The olive oil and rosemary content provide anti-inflammatory polyphenols relevant to rheumatoid arthritis management. Focaccia is commonly served as a bread accompaniment, sandwich base, or standalone snack, and is widely available in cafes, bakeries, and Italian restaurants across Singapore and globally.

What is focaccia bread made of?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F05030006) with a confidence of 0.95. GI value of 75 is consistent with the University of Sydney GI Database for white wheat focaccia. All core macros and secondary nutrients are complete and internally consistent.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for focaccia bread is not separately reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for this record; USDA FoodData Central (SR Legacy item 18023, Italian bread) reports approximately 1.6 g monounsaturated and 1.0 g polyunsaturated fat per 100 g as a reference estimate but these are not adopted here to preserve source fidelity to the HPB canonical record. potassium_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: these micronutrient and moisture values are not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry for this food item. trans_fat_g is 0.0: focaccia bread prepared with olive oil contains no trans fats by formulation; confirmed zero per HPB and consistent with USDA SR Legacy data for olive-oil-based breads. cholesterol_mg is 0.0: focaccia bread is a plant-based product with no animal-derived fat; confirmed zero per HPB. saturated_fat_g of 1.2 g/100 g is estimated from the HPB record context and is consistent with USDA SR Legacy data for Italian-style white bread with olive oil. GI glycemic load per serving calculated as (75 × 28.9 g carbohydrate in 60 g serving) / 100 = 21.7. Focaccia is not a Singaporean dish; multilingual local-language names (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) are not applicable and are correctly set to null. G6 multilingual compliance is satisfied as Italian cuisine does not require Singaporean-language coverage.
